interaction.text: A pile of gold coins, stacked and re-stacked by someone bored enough to count it twice.
A crate, empty but for straw packing and the smell of old rope.
A barrel with a cracked lid. Nothing inside but dust.
An urn, sealed, with a faded label you can't quite read anymore.
A skull sits on the shelf, jaw missing, watching nothing in particular.
Loose skeletal bones, stacked with more care than you'd expect.

Quartermaster's Orders

Never hide valuables with valuables. The guards always search the treasure first.

_incomplete: Converted from DUNGEON_NEW_AREA_4.tmj. All 5 tilesets already matched the project's known-good column counts (verified against true image dimensions and firstgid math) -- no crop-bug fixes needed, gids copied through unchanged. Collision = HORIZONTAL WALLS + SIDE WALLS + CRATES/CRATES 2/CRATES 3 + CHEST + TABLE, with the four doorway gaps (Kennels, Ossuary, Lower Stores, Treasury) cleared. Three of the four doors (Kennels/Ossuary/Treasury) render permanently open per the design doc ('three other doors are already open') -- only the Lower Stores door is a real gated door, using the same gid-swap animation pattern as Area 3's candle door and the Finale's grate. NEW MECHANIC: added a 'locked_door' interaction kind + matching 'door:unlock' server handler (mirrors the lever pattern exactly: checks the requesting player's own inventory for interactions.json's lockedDoors.door_area4_lower_stores.requiresItem, opens+persists the door zone-wide if found, tells just that player 'locked' otherwise) -- this project had no existing key-opens-door mechanic before this map needed one, so this is new game logic, not just content; worth a close look/playtest given it's untested. The key itself is the crate at (19,2) -- picked out from the ~37-cell CHEST layer because it alone uses distinct tile art (gids 641/642/653/654) from every other decoy crate cluster in the room, which all reuse a handful of repeating gids. The map's south half (rows 24-39, all four 'chambers') has no internal partition walls in the source data at all -- Kennels/Ossuary/Treasury/Lower-Stores are one contiguous open floor below the door band, distinguished only by name and doorway position, not by walls. The 'short corridor leading down the staircase into the maze' mentioned in the design doc isn't modelled by any distinct tiles either; exit_area4_forward is placed a few tiles south of the Lower Stores doorway as a reasonable stand-in and targets 'dungeon_area_5', which doesn't exist yet (same placeholder pattern as every other forward-exit in this project so far). The 50 unlabeled INTERACT POINTS objects (environmental flavour only, per the design doc) were given generic placeholder examine text cycling through 6 theme-appropriate lines (gold/crates/barrels/urns/skulls/bones) rather than 50 hand-written uniques, since no specific copy was provided for them -- worth swapping in real flavour text later if wanted. Not walked in-browser. Fixed during validation: HORIZONTAL WALLS row 20 (a uniform single-gid lip running the full width, no door cutouts anywhere) completely sealed the main room off from all four doors when taken as real collision -- excluded it, same as the decorative-row pattern found in Area 2's dais.
